summary: Iter 4: validated Twilio SMS no-op + JWT 24h + refresh_token endpoint + frontend refresh-flow. Backend 35/35 pytest passed (15 new tests for refresh + Twilio + 20 regression). Frontend: login stores both tokens, logout clears both, public landing/leads still work, brute-force lockout still trips at 6 (now keyed on email — iter-3 fix verified). ONE BUG FOUND: AdminDashboard.jsx synchronously checks getToken() and short-circuits to /admin/login before any API call is made — this defeats the axios silent-refresh interceptor in auth.js. With a valid refresh token but a missing/expired access token, the user is bounced to login instead of being transparently refreshed. The interceptor itself is wired correctly and /api/auth/refresh returns 200 — confirmed by direct fetch from the page.

integration_issues.issue: AdminDashboard.jsx (lines ~54 and ~105) does `const hasToken = Boolean(getToken())` and `if (!hasToken) return <Navigate to="/admin/login" replace />`. This synchronous guard runs BEFORE any API call, so when the access token is missing/expired but a valid refresh token still exists in localStorage, the user is redirected to /admin/login and the response interceptor in auth.js never gets a chance to call /api/auth/refresh. Verified directly: posting {refresh_token: localStorage.tnp_refresh} to /api/auth/refresh from the dashboard returns 200 + a new access_token, so the backend + auth.js interceptor are correct — the dashboard's synchronous gate is the only thing breaking the silent-refresh UX.

action_items: Fix AdminDashboard.jsx silent-refresh gate: instead of `if (!getToken()) <Navigate to=/admin/login>`, allow the component to mount whenever EITHER tnp_token OR tnp_refresh exists, and let the authAxios response interceptor handle the 401→refresh→retry. Suggested change: `const hasAnyToken = Boolean(getToken() || getRefreshToken()); if (!hasAnyToken) return <Navigate ...>`. The interceptor already handles the no-refresh case by clearing storage and redirecting.
Optional: cache JWT_SECRET at startup (currently re-read each call via _jwt_secret()).
Optional: tighten POST /api/leads broad try/except — pre-existing from iter 1.
critical_code_review_comments: notifications.py: implementation is clean — REQUIRED_ENV_VARS guard, lazy twilio import so missing package never breaks boot, _truncate to keep SMS body short, never logs the auth token (only sid/status/to). Confirmed by unit test test_notifications_logs_never_contain_auth_token which mocks twilio.rest.Client and asserts the secret token string never appears in any log record.

server.py: BackgroundTasks is correctly used so POST /api/leads returns immediately and SMS dispatch happens after response. Verified runtime log shows 'Twilio not configured — skipping SMS for lead <phone>' for every blank-env lead.
server.py: ACCESS_TOKEN_TTL_HOURS=24 and REFRESH_TOKEN_TTL_DAYS=30 confirmed via JWT decode (delta within ±5 min and ±1 hr respectively).

server.py /api/auth/refresh: correctly returns 401 for invalid token, expired token, and access-typed token (detail='Wrong token type' confirmed). Returns 422 for missing body. Refreshed access token authenticates against /auth/me.
auth.js interceptor logic is correct (single-flight refresh queue, isAuthCall guard to avoid loops, proper error path that clears storage). Bug is upstream in the dashboard guard.
AdminLogin.jsx correctly stores both data.access_token (setToken) and data.refresh_token (setRefreshToken) on success — verified in localStorage post-login.
Brute-force lockout: iter-3 fix verified. identifier='email:' + email so attempts now aggregate across pod IPs. 6th attempt = 429.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_refresh_and_sms.py (NEW — 15 tests covering JWT TTL, refresh endpoint, Twilio no-op, auth-token-not-logged)

success_rate.frontend: 75% (3/4 — login storage, logout-clears-both, public regression all PASS; silent-refresh interceptor flow FAIL due to dashboard guard)

seed_data_creation: Added ~6 TEST_Iter4_* leads via POST /api/leads (each triggered the 'Twilio not configured' no-op log). MongoDB now has ~21 TEST_ leads total. login_attempts cleared at start; ~6 entries from brute-force test will exist (unique bait email).
context_for_next_testing_agent: If main agent fixes the AdminDashboard.jsx guard, retest by: (1) login at /admin/login, (2) localStorage.removeItem('tnp_token') (keep tnp_refresh), (3) page.reload(), (4) assert URL stays at /admin and a new tnp_token is present. The /api/auth/refresh endpoint is fully working — direct fetch returns 200. Backend 35/35 pytest is the regression bar; rerun via: pytest backend/tests/ -v. Don't supply real Twilio creds; the no-op path + the mocked-Client unit test cover the full surface.
rca of the issue: Frontend silent-refresh RCA — AdminDashboard.jsx renders synchronously with `const hasToken = Boolean(getToken())` and returns `<Navigate to='/admin/login' replace />` when hasToken is falsy. React-Router executes this redirect before any useEffect/API call fires, so the authAxios response interceptor (which would catch a 401 and trigger /api/auth/refresh) never gets the opportunity to run. The interceptor logic in /app/frontend/src/lib/auth.js is correct — it has the single-flight queue, the isAuthCall guard against refresh loops, and proper error fallback. The fix is one-line at the dashboard guard: allow mount when tnp_token OR tnp_refresh exists, and rely on the interceptor for the actual refresh + redirect-on-failure. Reproduction: login → DevTools → Application → localStorage → delete `tnp_token` (keep `tnp_refresh`) → reload /admin → user is bounced to /admin/login (BAD). Expected: stays on /admin, dashboard re-fetches, interceptor mints new tnp_token, list renders. Verification of fix: same steps, plus assert localStorage.tnp_token is repopulated and is a valid 24h JWT.
